title: Domestic safety screening documentation

red_flags.text: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.
red_flags.rationale: The exact recognition section supports clinician awareness of immediate safety, injury, children, vulnerability, and escalation concerns without treating a generic screening field as a positive finding.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

exact_section.heading: Summary and Recognition of Domestic Abuse

examination.text: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.
examination.rationale: The exact WHO first-line-support section supports private, patient-led documentation of immediate emotional, physical, safety, and support needs without inferring violence, coercing disclosure, or generating an action.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook

examination.text: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.
examination.rationale: The exact history and examination section supports clinician-entered history and examination only when disclosure and consent make it appropriate; no intimate examination, assault, or injury finding is inferred.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook

exact_section.heading: History and physical examination after sexual violence

referral.text: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.
referral.rationale: The exact documentation section supports accurate clinician-entered records, available findings, patient words, consent-sensitive communication, safety discussion, and follow-up without automatic referral or reporting.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook

sources_used.title: Management of Domestic Abuse
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; requires UAE legal and safeguarding adaptation

sources_used.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
sources_used.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation

source_limitations: Disclosure status, immediate danger, children, injuries, sexual violence, capacity, consent, privacy, safeguarding, mandatory reporting, local services, referral, and escalation remain unsupported unless explicitly entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
